questions.text: Who are the 'Expert Detectives' in the story?
questions.explanation: Nishad (Seven) and his sister Maya consider themselves detectives investigating Mr. Nath.

questions.text: Why is Nishad called 'Seven'?
options: His name corresponds to the seventh note of the musical scale
questions.explanation: Nishad is a name for the seventh note on the Indian musical scale (Sa Re Ga Ma Pa Dha Ni).

questions.text: What is Maya's opinion of Mr. Nath?
options: He is a crook on the run
questions.explanation: Maya is convinced that Mr. Nath is a dangerous criminal hiding from the police.

questions.text: What is Nishad's initial feeling about Mr. Nath?
options: He thinks he is very rich
He thinks he is poor and starving
questions.explanation: Nishad notices Mr. Nath's thin appearance and thinks he doesn't have enough money for food.

questions.text: Who is the visitor who comes to see Mr. Nath every Sunday?
questions.explanation: A specific person visits Mr. Nath every Sunday afternoon for lunch.

questions.text: What does Ramesh tell the children about Mr. Nath's food habits?
options: He eats different things every day
He eats the same meal twice a day
questions.explanation: Ramesh, who brings the food, says Mr. Nath always has two chapattis, some dal, and a vegetable.

questions.text: What did Nishad give Mr. Nath when he went to his room?
questions.explanation: Nishad gave Mr. Nath a bar of chocolate out of sympathy for his condition.

questions.text: Why does Maya think Mr. Nath has scars on his face?
options: They are from a fire during a police shoot-out
questions.explanation: Maya imagines the scars are a result of a confrontation with the law.

questions.text: What does Mr. Nath pay Ramesh for the delivery?
options: He pays exactly the bill amount
He pays well and tips generously
questions.explanation: Ramesh mentions that Mr. Nath tips him well, which surprises Nishad.

questions.text: What was the purpose of the 'List of Facts' Maya wrote?
options: To show Nishad she was right
questions.explanation: Maya compiled a list of facts to logically prove Mr. Nath was a criminal.

questions.text: How did Maya and Nishad get into Mr. Nath's room initially?
options: Nishad's marble rolled into the room
questions.explanation: A marble rolling into the room gave Nishad a chance to see Mr. Nath.

questions.text: What does Mamma say about Mr. Nath's scars?
questions.explanation: Mamma, being a doctor, told them they were burn scars.

questions.text: What was Nishad's reaction to Maya's list of facts?
options: He was quiet and didn't seem to care about the 'facts'
questions.explanation: Nishad was only interested in whether Mr. Nath was lonely and poor, not Maya's detective work.

questions.text: Why does Maya think the Sunday visitor is an accomplice?
options: He brings the loot stolen by Mr. Nath
questions.explanation: Maya theorizes that the visitor keeps the money and brings part of it to Mr. Nath regularly.

questions.text: Which day did the children have off from school due to rain?
questions.explanation: The story mentions that the monsoon rains gave them an unexpected holiday on a Monday.

questions.text: What was Maya's title for her list of facts?
questions.explanation: She headed her paper 'CATCHING A CROOK' followed by the names of the detectives.

questions.text: Nishad says Mr. Nath is 'meek'. What does 'meek' mean?
questions.explanation: Meek describes someone who is quiet, gentle, and easily imposed upon.

questions.text: According to Maya, why does Mr. Nath not have any friends?
options: He is afraid someone might recognize him and tell the police
questions.explanation: Maya believes a criminal on the run cannot afford to have friends.

questions.text: What was the result of the argument between Maya and Nishad at the end?
options: They stopped speaking to each other
Nishad walked out of the room
questions.explanation: Nishad got fed up with Maya's theories and walked out, saying he wanted to be Mr. Nath's friend.

questions.text: What does Nishad want to find out about Mr. Nath?
options: Why he is so thin and why he is lonely
questions.explanation: Nishad's interest is purely based on empathy and human curiosity.

questions.text: Who is the author of 'Expert Detectives'?
questions.explanation: The story is an excerpt from 'The Broken Flute' by Sharada Dwivedi.

questions.text: What is the relation between Nishad and Maya?
questions.explanation: They are siblings who live with their mother.

questions.text: How many facts did Maya list in her notes?
questions.explanation: Maya numbered her facts from one to nine.

questions.text: Where does Mr. Nath live?
options: In a rented room at Shankar House
questions.explanation: He lived in one of the rooms at Shankar House.

questions.text: What does Ramesh bring for Mr. Nath every day from the restaurant?
questions.explanation: Ramesh brings two meals and tea (morning and afternoon).

questions.text: What is Mamma's profession?
questions.explanation: She is a doctor, and Mr. Nath is one of her patients.

questions.text: How long has Mr. Nath been living at Shankar House?
questions.explanation: Maya notes as Fact 1 that he has been there for over a year.

questions.text: Why do the other tenants at Shankar House think Mr. Nath is 'mad, strange and unfriendly'?
questions.explanation: His isolation and refusal to socialize makes people suspicious.

questions.text: Nishad was 'glaring' at Maya. What does 'glaring' mean?
questions.explanation: To glare is to stare in an angry or fierce way.

questions.text: What did Maya call her brother as a nickname?
questions.explanation: She consistently refers to him as Seven throughout their investigation.

questions.text: What does 'accomplice' mean in the context of the story?
questions.explanation: Maya uses this term to describe her theory about the Sunday visitor.

questions.text: Which of these is a 'fact' listed by Maya about Mr. Nath's behavior toward others?
questions.explanation: One of her points was that nobody ever writes to him.

questions.text: In Maya's view, how would the police catch Mr. Nath?
options: Wait for him to make a mistake
Search his room for the trunk of silver and gold
questions.explanation: She assumes he has a trunk full of treasure hidden in his room.

questions.text: What was Nishad doing while Maya was writing the facts?
options: Lying on his tummy and reading comics
questions.explanation: Nishad was indifferent to her writing and was busy with his comics.

questions.text: Maya believes Mr. Nath is a 'crook'. What is another word for 'crook'?
questions.explanation: A crook is an informal term for a criminal or a swindler.

questions.text: Why did Nishad feel sorry for Mr. Nath?
options: He looked lonely and had no friends
questions.explanation: Nishad's empathy is triggered by Mr. Nath's perceived loneliness.

questions.text: What color was the chocolate bar Nishad gave?
questions.explanation: The story mentions he thrust a bar of chocolate into his hand but doesn't specify the color.

questions.text: Nishad said, 'I'll go and find out why he is so thin'. This shows he is:
questions.explanation: Nishad's focus is on understanding and helping, unlike Maya's focus on judgment.

questions.text: What was the atmosphere at the beginning of the story?
options: A dark and stormy monsoon evening
questions.explanation: The story sets a mysterious tone with the rain and lightning.

questions.text: The conflict in the story is mainly between:
questions.explanation: The core of the story is the differing perspectives of the two children regarding the same person.
